title: Vendor call ledger line
description: One structured record of an external-vendor dispatch (a courier lane), written as a single newline-terminated JSON line to .agent-guild/state/log/vendor-calls.jsonl by ledger-append.py — no courier ever hand-formats this shape. Nullability on tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd, and brief_tokens means the vendor didn't report that figure; null is never replaced with a fabricated zero. tokenizer is null exactly when brief_tokens is null, and is the string "heuristic-bytes/4" whenever ledger-append.py computed brief_tokens itself (see --brief in ledger-append.py); this is a semantic rule the append helper enforces, since the conservative keyword set below can't express a cross-field constraint. Keyword set stays conservative like verdict.schema.json (type, properties, required, items, enum, additionalProperties) so validate-verdict.py's hand-rolled validator pattern covers this schema too.

task_id.description: The guild task this vendor call served, e.g. "T-007".

vendor.description: The external vendor dispatched to, e.g. "codex".

model.description: The model the vendor ran, e.g. "gpt-5.5".

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the call started, e.g. "2026-07-22T18:00:00Z".

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the call, in milliseconds.

tokens_in.description: Input tokens the vendor reported, or null if the vendor didn't report a figure — never a fabricated 0.

tokens_out.description: Output tokens the vendor reported, or null if the vendor didn't report a figure — never a fabricated 0.

cost_usd.description: Cost in US dollars the vendor reported, or null if the vendor didn't report a figure — never a fabricated 0.

brief_tokens.description: Size of the brief handed to the vendor, in tokens, or null if no brief was measured. When present it is a stdlib heuristic (bytes of the brief file divided by 4), not a vendor-reported or vendor-tokenizer figure — see tokenizer.

tokenizer.description: Name of the tokenizer that produced brief_tokens ("heuristic-bytes/4" for the stdlib byte-count heuristic), or null exactly when brief_tokens is null.

artifacts.description: Paths the courier verified on disk after the call, never paths the vendor merely claimed to have written. May be empty but must be present.

quota_event.description: True when this call registered a vendor quota exhaustion. A quota_event line is always written before any exhaustion sentinel is touched, so the ledger always explains the sentinel.
